comments_critique.summary: The discussion revolves around nostalgia for the Drugwars game on TI calculators, sharing personal experiences, technical challenges, and related projects. Participants express enthusiasm, reminisce, and discuss technical aspects of programming and hardware.

comments.author: jckahn
comments.text:
This game is a really big deal for me! I was addicted to it in high school and it left a lasting impression. Drugwars directly inspired my passion project, Farmhand: https://www.farmhand.life/

I'm so happy to see this pop up here! :)
comments.analysis: This comment provides a personal and emotional perspective, with a credible connection to the game's influence on the author's projects.

comments.author: firesteelrain
comments.text: I wish I had a cable to download these games or even a unit to unit cable. I hand typed them into my TI-82.
comments.analysis: The comment reflects a common nostalgic frustration and personal experience, adding credibility to the discussion.

comments.author: mrhyyyyde
comments.text: First experiences around programming were on an 83, I'll never forget those choose your own adventure games I let friends play in class.
comments.analysis: Provides a personal anecdote that aligns with the era and context, adding depth to the nostalgic theme.

comments.author: BewareTheYiga
comments.text: The amount of classroom time I wasted playing this game…
comments.analysis: Expresses a common sentiment about gaming in class, contributing to the nostalgic tone but less technical detail.

comments.author: apatheticonion
comments.text: Can someone please compile this to wasm? I'd love to play this again
comments.analysis: Requests technical action; plausible but less verifiable without further context.

comments.author: zeckalpha
comments.text: coincidentally, a SilverLink cable arrived here today so I can program my 85 and 83 Plus.
comments.analysis: Details a technical setup; credible but less directly related to the original game discussion.

comments.author: gurkin
comments.analysis: A brief, non-informative comment that doesn't add substantive analysis.

comments.author: dietrichepp
comments.text: I remember playing this, but also a puzzle game called “dstar”, which I ported to the web: https://www.moria.us/games/dstar/play
comments.analysis: Mentions a personal project related to the topic, adding some credibility but less depth.
